name: Darius Slayton
headline: A deep-shot specialist buried in a crowded Giants room
body:
Slayton remains exactly what the advanced numbers say he is: an efficient field-stretcher who never commands enough volume to matter weekly. In 2025 he ran 395 routes across 14 games on an 81.3% snap share, yet earned just a 12.5% target share and a 0.159 target-per-route rate, finishing WR55 with only 61.8 fantasy points. The vertical role still produces juice, 13.4 air yards per target, 22.8 air yards per reception, and a healthy 0.252 EPA per play, but a 58.7% catch rate, five drops, and a lone touchdown cap the ceiling.

The 2026 setup only tightens the squeeze. With Malik Nabers entrenched and Darnell Mooney plus Isaiah Likely added, Slayton drops to a clear fourth or fifth option, and he's working back from offseason sports hernia surgery. A modest projection near 35-520-2 fits a player drafted around WR100. Treat him as a bye-week dart and waiver-wire stash, nothing more.
